title: angelica keiskei
intro: Angelica keiskei, also known as Mokuru or Japanese angelica, is a perennial plant native to East Asia, characterized by its large, fern-like leaves and tall, hollow stems. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to reduce inflammation and act as an antiseptic, antispasmodic, and diuretic. The health benefits of Angelica keiskei include relief from menstrual cramps, arthritis, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential to boost the immune system and lower blood pressure. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
